title: Status page / uptime page
description: Data contract for the status-page format: a service status / uptime page. The page is a current-status BANNER (label + colour DERIVED from the worst CURRENT component state), per-component UPTIME-HISTORY day-bar strips grouped into component groups (with a 90/60/30-day window selector that re-slices every strip and recomputes the period uptime %), an 'only issues' filter and group filter chips, a PAST-INCIDENTS feed grouped by date (each incident card expands a timestamped update timeline: investigating -> identified -> monitoring -> resolved), and a SCHEDULED-MAINTENANCE section. This is uptime HISTORY + incident COMMS — it is NOT the dashboard (no live-metric KPI tiles) and NOT the incident-timeline (that is one incident's minute-by-minute postmortem chronology). The banner label/colour, every period uptime %, and the day-bar colours are DERIVED from the data, never hard-coded. The #report-data instance in status_page_sample.html is the canonical example. Default theme: cyan.

meta.description: Shared report identity block (see _DATA_DRIVEN.md). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}}/{{PROJECT}}/{{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

org.description: Organisation wordmark shown in the masthead + footer. Placeholder: {{ORG}}.

project.description: Project / platform whose status is reported. Placeholder: {{PROJECT}}.

reportType.description: Artefact type label shown in the masthead sub-line.

title.description: Status-page title shown in the masthead.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line description of what this status page covers.

date.description: Report generation date (YYYY-MM-DD). Shown in the footer; distinct from the live 'asOf' timestamp.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; set on <html data-theme>. Live-switchable via the .rc-theme dropdown (9 presets incl. Black). Default cyan for this format.

asOf.description: Optional 'as of' timestamp shown in the masthead (ISO-8601 with offset). The freshness anchor for the current-status banner. Falls back to meta.date if omitted.

windows.description: The selectable uptime-history windows (in days), rendered as the window-selector buttons. The largest value determines how many day-bars each component renders; smaller values slice the trailing N. Conventionally [90, 60, 30].

groups.description: Component GROUPS. Each group renders as a panel containing the component rows whose groupId matches. Also drives the group filter chips. Render order is top-to-bottom.

id.description: Stable unique group key; referenced by each component's groupId and by the group filter chips.

name.description: Group display name shown in the group panel header.

components.description: The monitored components, one row each under their group panel. Each row = a status dot + name + a horizontal strip of N day-bars (one per day; colour = that day's worst state) + the period uptime % (derived). Brand-neutral platform pieces (API, Auth, Payments, Notifications, Batch, DB, etc.).

id.description: Stable unique component key; referenced by incidents[].components and maintenance[].components, and used to pick the row icon.

groupId.description: The id of the group this component belongs to (must match a groups[].id).

description.description: Optional one-line description shown in mono beneath the component name.

days.description: The per-day uptime history for this component, OLDEST first, one entry per day. Length should cover the largest value in `windows` (e.g. 90 entries). Each entry colours one day-bar and feeds the period uptime %. (The shipped sample uses the string sentinel "__GEN__" to have the renderer synthesise a reproducible 90-day history aligned with the incident feed; a real instance supplies the array verbatim.)

date.description: The calendar day this bar represents (YYYY-MM-DD).

uptime.description: Uptime percentage for the day (0-100). Averaged across the selected window to compute the period uptime %.

state.description: The day's worst observed state; sets the day-bar colour. 'ok' = operational (ok colour); 'hold' = degraded (amber); 'risk' = outage (bordeaux).

incidents.description: Optional count of incidents that touched this component on this day; shown in the day-bar hover tooltip.

oneOf.description: Sentinel used by the sample only: the renderer generates a reproducible (seeded) day history aligned with the incidents feed. Not for production instances.

incidents.description: The PAST-INCIDENTS feed, grouped by date (newest first). Each incident is a card with a severity badge, impacted-component chips, and a vertical list of timestamped updates. Aligns with the components' day history (an incident's day shows as hold/risk on the affected components' strips).

date.description: The date the incident occurred (YYYY-MM-DD); the feed groups cards under this date heading.

examples: Elevated error rate on Payments authorisation

severity.description: Incident severity, drives the badge colour and the implied day-bar state: 'major'/'partial' -> risk (bordeaux, outage); 'degraded'/'minor' -> hold (amber).

status.description: Current lifecycle state. When 'resolved', the card badge renders in the ok colour regardless of severity.

components.description: The ids of components this incident impacted (each must match a components[].id). Rendered as chips on the card.

updates.description: The chronological status updates posted during the incident (the .sp-update timeline). The renderer shows them newest-first within the card. Each update's status colours its timeline node.

time.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (with offset) of the update.

status.description: The update's lifecycle stage; colours the timeline node. investigating -> risk, identified -> hold, monitoring -> accent, resolved -> ok.

maintenance.description: Scheduled maintenance windows, rendered in the Scheduled Maintenance section. Each is a card with the time window, the impacted-component chips, and a description.

window.description: The maintenance window as an ISO-8601 interval 'start/end' (both with offset). The renderer formats the date + start–end time.

components.description: The ids of components affected by the maintenance (each must match a components[].id). Rendered as chips.

body.description: Description of the maintenance and its expected impact.

meta.title: Platform Status
meta.subtitle: Live operational status, 90-day uptime history, and incident communications for the {{PROJECT}} platform.
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

components.name: API Gateway
components.description: Public REST / GraphQL ingress and routing.

components.name: Payments
components.description: Authorisation, capture, and settlement of transactions.

incidents.title: Elevated error rate on Payments authorisation

updates.body: We are investigating elevated 5xx responses on the Payments authorisation endpoint.
A connection-pool exhaustion was identified after a downstream datastore slowdown; pool limits raised.
Error rate has returned to baseline; monitoring for recurrence before resolving.

maintenance.title: Primary Database engine upgrade
maintenance.body: The primary database will be upgraded to the next major engine version; a brief failover is expected near the start of the window.
